truth.papers.A31.title: Influence of Al content and precipitation state on the mechanical behavior of austenitic high-Mn low-density steels
truth.papers.A31.paper_scope: To clarify the role of precipitation state in the strain hardening mechanisms of low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steels by analyzing deformation structures of two alloys with different Al contents.

prediction.papers.A31.title: Influence of Al content and precipitation state on the mechanical behavior of austenitic high-Mn low-density steels
prediction.papers.A31.paper_scope: Investigation of the effect of Al content and precipitation state on strain hardening mechanisms and mechanical behavior in austenitic high-Mn low-density steels under room-temperature tensile deformation.

truth.processes.proc_2al_short.description: Induction melted and cast into round bars (25 mm diameter); reheated to 1200 °C for 30 min; hot-rolled at 1100 °C with 75% thickness reduction, water quenched; solution treated at 1100 °C for 2 h under Ar, water quenched; short annealed at 450 °C for 1 h.
truth.processes.proc_2al_long.description: Induction melted and cast into round bars (25 mm diameter); reheated to 1200 °C for 30 min; hot-rolled at 1100 °C with 75% thickness reduction, water quenched; solution treated at 1100 °C for 2 h under Ar, water quenched; long annealed at 650 °C for 72 h.
truth.processes.proc_8al_short.description: Induction melted and cast into round bars (25 mm diameter); reheated to 1200 °C for 30 min; hot-rolled at 1100 °C with 75% thickness reduction, water quenched; solution treated at 1100 °C for 2 h under Ar, water quenched; short annealed at 450 °C for 1 h.
truth.processes.proc_8al_long.description: Induction melted and cast into round bars (25 mm diameter); reheated to 1200 °C for 30 min; hot-rolled at 1100 °C with 75% thickness reduction, water quenched; solution treated at 1100 °C for 2 h under Ar, water quenched; long annealed at 600 °C for 24 h.

prediction.processes.proc_2al_short.description: Melted and cast to 25 mm round bars, reheated to 1200°C for 30 min, hot-rolled to 75% thickness reduction at 1100°C with water quench, solution-treated at 1100°C for 2 h under Ar with water quench, then short-annealed at 450°C for 1 h.
prediction.processes.proc_2al_long.description: Melted and cast to 25 mm round bars, reheated to 1200°C for 30 min, hot-rolled to 75% thickness reduction at 1100°C with water quench, solution-treated at 1100°C for 2 h under Ar with water quench, then long-annealed at 650°C for 72 h.
prediction.processes.proc_8al_short.description: Melted and cast to 25 mm round bars, reheated to 1200°C for 30 min, hot-rolled to 75% thickness reduction at 1100°C with water quench, solution-treated at 1100°C for 2 h under Ar with water quench, then short-annealed at 450°C for 1 h.
prediction.processes.proc_8al_long.description: Melted and cast to 25 mm round bars, reheated to 1200°C for 30 min, hot-rolled to 75% thickness reduction at 1100°C with water quench, solution-treated at 1100°C for 2 h under Ar with water quench, then long-annealed at 600°C for 24 h.

truth.structures.sample_2al_short.overall_stru: Austenitic (γ) solid solution; after tensile straining, multiple hardening stages with dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, cells, cell blocks) and deformation twinning.
truth.structures.sample_2al_short.microstructu: deformed grains containing dislocation substructures and twins
dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, cells, cell blocks) and deformation twins
truth.structures.sample_2al_long.overall_struc: Grain interior: dislocation cell blocks (avg ~275 ± 50 nm at true strain 0.2); grain boundaries: intergranular M3C-type carbides (~1 μm wide × 3 μm long); deformation twins interact with carbides promoting intergranular fracture.
truth.structures.sample_2al_long.microstructur: grain interior with dislocation cell blocks
truth.structures.sample_8al_short.overall_stru: After short annealing: heterogeneous precipitation of nanosized κ-carbides (2-5 nm, L'12-type) in austenite; deformation structure (true strain 0.3): planar dislocation configurations (Taylor lattice structures and highly dense dislocation walls, HDDWs, spaced 100-200 nm).
truth.structures.sample_8al_short.microstructu: deformed grains with planar slip bands
cube/cube orientation relationship with austenite matrix
truth.structures.sample_8al_long.overall_struc: Long annealing produces uniform distribution of cuboidal κ-carbides (10-20 nm) with narrow matrix channels (~2-3 nm); deformation (true strain 0.15) develops planar dislocation arrays spaced 100-300 nm, with sheared κ-carbides.
truth.structures.sample_8al_long.microstructur: matrix channels between precipitates, deformed by planar slip
planar dislocation arrays (dense dislocation walls spaced 100-300 nm)
cube/cube orientation relationship with austenite matrix, sheared by dislocations

prediction.structures.sample_2al_short.overall_stru: Solid solution austenite (γ phase). Under tensile deformation, exhibits multiple-stage strain hardening: first stage governed by dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, dislocation cells, cell blocks); second stage activated by deformation twinning at high stress levels (~950 MPa true stress).
prediction.structures.sample_2al_short.microstructu: twin spacing determined by twinning kinetics
dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, dislocation cells, cell blocks)
prediction.structures.sample_2al_long.overall_struc: Grain interior remains essentially solid solution austenite (γ phase); grain boundaries and annealing twin interfaces contain intergranular M3C-type carbides. Under deformation, dislocation cell blocks develop at 0.2 true strain; twin bundles form at intermediate-to-high stress and impinge on carbides, triggering intergranular fracture.
prediction.structures.sample_2al_long.microstructur: intergranular, about 1 μm wide × 3 μm long
cell block size at 0.2 true strain
uniform along grain boundaries and annealing twin interfaces
prediction.structures.sample_8al_short.overall_stru: Austenite matrix with heterogeneous distribution of intragranular nanosized j-carbides. Under deformation, planar dislocation configurations develop (Taylor lattice structures and highly dense dislocation walls, HDDWs, spaced 100–200 nm); j-carbides are crystallographically sheared by dislocations without pinning.
prediction.structures.sample_8al_short.microstructu: cube/cube orientation relationship with austenite matrix
prediction.structures.sample_8al_long.overall_struc: Austenite matrix with uniform distribution of intragranular cuboidal j-carbides and narrow matrix channels (~2–3 nm). Under deformation, planar dislocation substructures develop with dense dislocation arrays spaced 100–300 nm; cuboidal j-carbides are frequently sheared by dislocations.
prediction.structures.sample_8al_long.microstructur: cube/cube orientation relationship with austenite matrix

truth.interfaces.sample_2al_long.defect_intera: twin tip impingement on intergranular M3C carbide
Local shear stress concentration at carbide interfaces, promoting intergranular fracture
truth.interfaces.sample_2al_long.interface_not: Stress concentration at twin–carbide interfaces triggers damage.

prediction.interfaces.sample_2al_long.defect_intera: deformation twin impingement on intergranular M3C carbides
produces local shear stress concentrations at carbide interfaces, enhancing intergranular fracture mode
prediction.interfaces.sample_2al_long.phase_evoluti: Segregation of solute elements (mainly carbon) to grain boundaries results in precipitation of coarse M3C-type carbides during long annealing; in the grain boundary region the phase evolution is γ → γ0 + intergranular M3C-type carbides.
prediction.interfaces.sample_8al_short.defect_inter: no dislocation-particle pinning events observed; j-carbides act as weak obstacles and are crystallographically sheared due to their tiny size
prediction.interfaces.sample_8al_long.defect_intera: cuboidal j-carbides are frequently sheared by dislocations within dislocation arrays; plasticity is mainly concentrated through the dislocation boundaries of planar substructures

prediction.properties.sample_2al_short.mechanical.t: about 2.6 GPa at early deformation stage; 5.0 GPa at 400 MPa true stress decreasing to ~1.5 GPa at 1800 MPa true stress GPa
prediction.properties.sample_2al_long.mechanical.te: overlaps first hardening regime of short-annealed sample up to ~1 GPa true stress; 5.0 GPa at 400 MPa decreasing to ~2.1 GPa at 1000 MPa GPa
prediction.properties.sample_8al_short.mechanical.t: about 2 GPa at early deformation stage; single hardening regime with 5 GPa at 400 MPa dropping to 1 GPa at 1600 MPa GPa
prediction.properties.sample_8al_long.mechanical.te: single hardening regime, lower than short-annealed 8Al; 5 GPa at 600 MPa, maintaining ~2 GPa up to 1200 MPa then dropping to 1 GPa at 1600 MPa GPa

prediction.performance.sample_2al_short.comparative: UTS higher by 85 MPa (935 vs 850 MPa); uniform elongation improved from 35% to 75%
prediction.performance.sample_8al_short.comparative: YS increased from 420 MPa to 540 MPa (about 29% increase) due to sheared nanosized j-carbides
prediction.performance.sample_8al_long.comparative_: YS increased from 420 MPa to 990 MPa (about 136% increase); UTS 1180 MPa with UE 37%

truth.characterization_methods.sample_2al_long: Observation of deformation structures and fracture surface
Dislocation cell blocks average 275 ± 50 nm at true strain 0.2; uniform intergranular M3C carbides (1 μm wide, 3 μm long); deformation twin–carbide interactions lead to intergranular fracture.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_8al_shor: Heterogeneous distribution of intragranular nanosized κ-carbides (2-5 nm, L'12-type), cube/cube orientation with austenite; no dislocation–particle pinning observed.
truth.characterization_methods.sample_8al_long: Uniform cuboidal κ-carbides (10-20 nm) with cube/cube orientation; sheared particles within dislocation arrays.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_2al_shor: characterize deformation microstructures (dislocations, twins) at different strain levels under controlled diffraction conditions
Dislocation substructures (Taylor lattice, cells, cell blocks) at early stage; deformation twin bundles at high stress (~950 MPa true stress).
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_2al_long: characterize deformation microstructures, carbide distribution and fracture mode
Dislocation cell blocks ~275 ± 50 nm at 0.2 true strain; intergranular M3C carbides ~1 μm × 3 μm along grain boundaries; twin–carbide interactions leading to intergranular fracture.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_8al_shor: analyze precipitation state, particle size and diffraction patterns
Heterogeneous intragranular nanosized j-carbides 2–5 nm with L1_0 structure and cube/cube orientation relationship with austenite matrix; no dislocation–particle pinning observed.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_8al_long: analyze precipitation state and particle–dislocation interactions
Uniform intragranular cuboidal j-carbides 10 × 20 nm with narrow matrix channels ~2–3 nm; frequent shearing of cuboidal j-carbides by dislocations.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Applied to 2Al steel to describe strain hardening contributions
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Flow stress contribution from dislocation substructure size
Δσ = G M K b / D; G: shear modulus, K: constant, b: Burgers vector, M: Taylor factor, D: dislocation substructure size
Used for first hardening stage in 2Al steel.
Δσ_twinning = K_H-P / (λ_twin)^(1/2); K_H-P: constant, λ_twin: average twin spacing
Describes second hardening stage in 2Al steel.

prediction.computational_details.sample_2al_short.a: contribution of dislocation substructure to flow stress in the first hardening stage
G (shear modulus), K (constant), b (Burgers vector magnitude), M (Taylor factor), D (substructure size)
Δσ = G M K b / D; high carbon content promotes formation of dislocation substructures by influencing cross-slip.
contribution of deformation twinning to hardening in the second hardening stage
Δσ_twinning = K_H-P / (λ_twin)^1/2; twin spacing is the key microstructural parameter determined by twinning kinetics.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Twinning stress estimated for 2Al steel: σ_twinning ~ γ/b = 670-750 MPa (assuming γ = 60 mJ/m², b = 0.25 nm, Taylor factor 2.8-3.2).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Long annealed 2Al steel contains intergranular incoherent M3C-type carbides; grain interior remains essentially solid solution (γ phase).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: In long annealed 8Al steel, high volume fraction of κ-carbides creates narrow matrix channels 2-3 nm wide.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Short annealed 8Al steel shows planar slip enhancement due to shearable nanosized κ-carbides.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Figure 1 provides engineering stress–strain curves for annealed 2Al and 8Al steels; the caption includes a table of data points, but the actual curve shapes and pixel-level image data are unavailable in the text pipeline.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 2 shows strain hardening rate versus true stress for annealed 2Al steels; a data table is present in the caption text, but the plot image is not available.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Figure 4 shows strain hardening rate versus true stress for annealed 8Al steels; a data table is present in the caption text, but the plot image is not available.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Microscopic images in Figures 3 and 5 (ECCI and TEM micrographs) contain only scale-bar information and qualitative descriptions in the caption; pixel-level microstructural details are unavailable.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: For the long-annealed 2Al steel, the estimated twinning stress is ~670–750 MPa based on stacking fault energy γ = 60 mJ/m², b = 0.25 nm, and Taylor factor 2.8–3.2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The short-annealed 2Al steel develops planar dislocation configurations at low stresses due to the high carbon content influencing dislocation cross-slip; these transform into wavy dislocation structures (cells and cell blocks) at high true stress (~950 MPa), whereas in the short-annealed 8Al alloy planar dislocation substructures remain stable up to ~1.1 GPa because of shearable j-carbides.
